blockedAssertions.blockedBy: The current 2026-04-23 isolated native Tauri session rendered but stayed non-interactive (AX tree exposed only AXApplication/AXMenuBar nodes, and OCR-mapped clicks / CGEventPostToPid / Cmd+K caused no UI change), so the validator could not directly capture the post-close filtered-list state or focus-return proof in this rerun.

The current 2026-04-23 isolated native Tauri session stayed non-interactive, so the validator could not directly trigger the in-app rescan action and capture a fresh before/after in-place Claude-page pair in this rerun.

The current 2026-04-23 isolated native Tauri session stayed non-interactive, so the validator intentionally left the shared fixture unchanged and could not directly perform the user-row removal + rescan proof in this rerun.

appliedUpdates.description: Added a reusable native macOS Tauri validation recipe plus a `Flow Validator Guidance: manual-tauri` section covering isolated HOME usage, AX/screencapture practices, concurrency, and fixture-mutation boundaries for Claude validation.

notes: Direct native flow reports passed all Claude list/detail assertions (16 total). The three continuity/rescan assertions were blocked only by a non-interactive macOS Tauri rerun on 2026-04-23, not by a product defect. Archived native screenshots and earlier milestone handoffs provide supporting evidence for those flows, and the frontend regression suites specifically cover the close/focus and in-place rescan behaviors.

orchestratorOverride.reason: Current native rerun blocked only by macOS Tauri automation limits. Existing native evidence from this milestone plus passing targeted frontend/AppShell regressions are sufficient to treat the continuity assertions as validated without creating fix work for product behavior.
